name: Nana Akosua Afrakomah Addai-Mensah
role: Founder & Team Lead, Terra Bonum Enterprise
bio:
I'm Nana Akosua Afrakomah Addai-Mensah, founder of Terra Bonum Enterprise.

Before kente, I spent over twenty years in Ghana's banking and finance sector — building loan portfolios, managing risk, and training teams to deliver under pressure. I hold a BSc in Biochemistry and an MBA in Finance, and early in my career I even helped a major beverage company earn its ISO 9001 quality certification. None of it had anything to do with weaving.

In 2019, I set that career aside to build something different: a kente production business rooted in the traditions of Bonwire, run with the same discipline I'd spent two decades learning. Today, Terra Bonum supports a team of 40 weavers, and I'm personally involved in every part of it — from quality control on the loom to the order that ships to your door.

When you buy from Terra Bonum, you're not buying from a faceless shop. You're buying from someone who has staked her name, her standards, and her career on getting this right — and who is proud to put a genuine Ghanaian craft, and the livelihoods behind it, into your hands.
